challenges.title: Optimize Nested Loops
challenges.description: Find and fix performance issues in this code. It finds duplicate elements in an array but has serious performance problems.
learning_objectives: Learn to avoid unnecessary nested loops
hints: The nested loop has O(n²) time complexity
List membership check is O(n), set is O(1)
Consider using set() for efficient lookups
challenges.solution_hint: Use a set to store seen elements to reduce time complexity from O(n³) to O(n)

challenges.title: Fix Security Vulnerabilities
challenges.description: Find security vulnerabilities in this code. It handles user input and has serious security risks.
learning_objectives: Learn the importance of parameterized queries
hints: eval() can execute arbitrary Python code - very dangerous
SQL string concatenation leads to SQL injection vulnerabilities
Consider using ast.literal_eval() instead of eval()
Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection
challenges.solution_hint: Use ast.literal_eval() instead of eval(), use parameterized queries instead of string concatenation

challenges.title: Reduce Cyclomatic Complexity
challenges.description: Refactor this high complexity code. The nesting is too deep, making it hard to read and maintain.
learning_objectives: Learn to simplify conditional logic with operators
hints: Consider using early return pattern
Nested conditions can be merged into single expressions
Use and/or operators to simplify nested if statements
Consider configuration objects when parameters are too many
challenges.solution_hint: Use early returns to reduce nesting, merge conditional expressions, extract functions to reduce complexity

challenges.title: Fix Code Smells
challenges.description: Identify and fix bad practices in this code. It has multiple code smell issues.
learning_objectives: Understand the harm of dead code
Learn to clean up unused variables and parameters
hints: Is the number of parameters reasonable? Suggest no more than 5
Are there unused variables? data, result, multiple parameters
Does iterating over an empty list make sense?
temp = temp + x can be simplified to temp += x
challenges.solution_hint: Remove unused parameters and variables, simplify function signature

challenges.title: Performance Optimization
challenges.description: Optimize this inefficient data processing code. It performs poorly with large datasets.
learning_objectives: Learn to use sets for deduplication
hints: Strings are immutable in Python, each concatenation creates a new object
Using str.join() is more efficient
List membership check is O(n), set is O(1)
challenges.solution_hint: Use ','.join() instead of loop concatenation, use set() for deduplication

challenges.title: Exception Handling Best Practices
challenges.description: Identify and fix improper exception handling. Good exception handling is the foundation of robust programs.
learning_objectives: Understand the dangers of bare except
hints: Bare except: catches all exceptions including KeyboardInterrupt
File operations should use 'with' statement for proper resource release
Should catch specific exception types, not generic Exception
eval() is not the correct way to parse JSON
challenges.solution_hint: Use 'with' statement, catch specific exception types, use json.loads() instead of eval()

challenges.title: Recursion Optimization
challenges.description: Optimize this inefficient recursive function. It calculates Fibonacci numbers but is extremely slow.
hints: fibonacci(n) has O(2^n) time complexity
Same subproblems are calculated repeatedly
Consider using cache to store calculated results
Python's @functools.lru_cache decorator is very useful
challenges.solution_hint: Use lru_cache decorator or implement memoization manually, or use iterative approach

challenges.title: Type Safety Improvement
challenges.description: Improve type safety of this code. It lacks type hints and is error-prone.
learning_objectives: Understand benefits of using Enum instead of magic strings
hints: Use typing module to add type hints
Consider using Union, Optional, Any types
Use Enum instead of magic strings
Avoid bare except, use specific exception types
challenges.solution_hint: Add type hints, use Enum to define operation types, improve exception handling

challenges.title: Memory Optimization
challenges.description: Optimize memory usage. This code consumes a lot of memory when processing large data.
learning_objectives: Learn to process large files line by line
hints: readlines() loads entire file into memory at once
Can iterate over file object directly for line-by-line reading
Consider using generator expressions instead of lists
yield keyword creates generator functions
challenges.solution_hint: Use generators to read files line by line, avoid loading all content at once

challenges.title: Concurrency Safety
challenges.description: Identify potential issues in concurrent programming. This code has race conditions in multi-threaded environments.
hints: increment() method is not atomic
Read-modify-write process can be interrupted
Singleton lazy loading is also thread-unsafe
Consider using threading.Lock or threading.RLock
challenges.solution_hint: Use locks to protect shared resources, use thread-safe singleton implementation

challenges.title: Code Testability
challenges.description: Improve code testability. This code is difficult to unit test.
learning_objectives: Master how to decouple external dependencies
hints: Direct calls to requests.get() are hard to mock in tests
datetime.now() is hard to control in tests
Function does too many things, violates Single Responsibility Principle
Consider passing dependencies as parameters or using interface abstraction
challenges.solution_hint: Use dependency injection for external services, extract interfaces for easier mocking

challenges.title: Data Class Optimization
challenges.description: Improve this class using modern Python features. It can be implemented more elegantly.
hints: Python 3.7+ provides @dataclass decorator
dataclass auto-generates __init__, __eq__, __repr__
Can use asdict() function to convert to dictionary
Consider adding type hints for better code quality
challenges.solution_hint: Use @dataclass decorator to simplify class definition, auto-generate special methods

categories.name: Performance
categories.description: Learn to identify and optimize inefficient code

categories.name: Security
categories.description: Identify and fix common security issues

categories.name: Complexity
categories.description: Reduce code complexity to improve maintainability
